The pack makes no production_ready overclaim: manifest and command-evidence both record production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review, with the production_ready_flag being a descriptor-verification label and Hermes production_ready_candidate reflecting only coverage.valid && slice.valid; final readiness is deferred to human approval and downstream runtime-opening pack CP00-298. Deliverable distribution (claude_review:6, security_audit:4, test:2, ui:2, implementation:26 = 40) and micro-title distribution (20/20) match the baseline. All review rows are descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / raw_payload_included:false; the command_rerun guard (executes_command_runtime:false) is correctly inherited via the unbroken CP281->...->CP296->CP297 row-extras spread chain, so validator checks pass legitimately. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive (synthetic_only, no_real_data, no DB rows, no object storage, no audit-event writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command execution). Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required:true, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling, not embedded product runtime. CP178+ hardening is honored via forbidden_review_evidence (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) and a passing auth preflight with valid JSON. Contract projections (current_pack, cp297_requirements, cp297_no_write_attestation, review_slice_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings tail=CP00-297) are statically consistent with the code-generated structures deep-equaled by the validator; index.js wildcard re-exports propagate the new exports; tests cover coverage/descriptor/Hermes/Claude/handoff; README documents the pack.
